title: Generate Google Docs
excerpt: I need to generate Google Docs from Monday.com via an integration. Monday’s integration with Zapier, Integromat, etc. is no good. I’d like to be able to populate a Google Doc with column data from my Monday boards; this …

posts.author: Simonga25 (George Simon)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need to generate Google Docs from Monday.com via an integration. Monday’s integration with Zapier, Integromat, etc. is no good. I’d like to be able to populate a Google Doc with column data from my Monday boards; this would require similar functionality to the Gmail integration.

I need this automate my content production system for content marketing.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Simonga25 - Take a look at the DocuGen app in the Marketplace: https://monday.com/marketplace/12

posts.author: max (max)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Simonga25

DocuGen is a good plug and play option, though they recently stopped supporting Google Docs for some reason. To their credit, they have excellent customer service, and if you’re okay with using Word instead, it could be a good option.

I struggled with Zapier for a while, and was pretty disappointed. Integromat on the other hand has been amazing! There’s a bit of a learning curve, but once you get the hang of it, you can make it do almost anything.

There’s a really easy Google Docs module that can generate docs from templates, filling in variables with content from Monday. I suggest using “Webhooks” as your trigger, which will shoot your item ID to Integromat, then use a Monday module to get the item, then use that Google Docs module to create a new doc mapping whatever content you want from Monday.

Hope this helps

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml:
@Simonga25 @PolishedGeek It’s been a while, but I haven’t forgotten!
I got a solution for you to generate Google Doc using DocuGen in 3 easy steps with the help of Zapier:

DocuGen help center

Save generated documents to Google Drive

You can have DocuGen automatically save generated documents to your Google Drive. Here is how: Create a Zapier email inbox Set up Zapier to save email attachments to your Google Drive Tell DocuGen...

Small tweak: you need to make sure the Zapier settings are set to convert the Word doc to Google Doc format.

Let me know if it works!
